title: kql_edr_killer_hunt.kql
description: EDR Killer hunt queries for Microsoft Sentinel / Defender for Endpoint (MDE) These queries hunt for the "kill-don't-evade" behavioral pattern: vulnerable driver load → EDR process termination → credential harvest Tables used: DeviceEvents           — MDE telemetry (driver load, process termination) DeviceProcessEvents    — process creation + termination SecurityEvent          — Windows Security Log
content:
// Source: https://github.com/AndrewAltimit/exploits
// Collected: 2026-05-10
// KQL Hunter - https://github.com/YOUR_USER/kql-hunter

let DriverLoadEvents = DeviceEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType == "DriverLoad"
    | extend DriverPath = tostring(AdditionalFields.DriverPath)
    | where DriverPath has_any (
        "RTCore64", "gdrv", "DBUtil_2_3", "WinRing0x64",
        "LenovoHECI", "MsIo64", "AsIO64", "InpoutX64",
        "Speedfan", "GDrv3", "AsrDrv106", "RtkHDAud64"
    )
    | project DriverLoadTime=Timestamp, DeviceName, DriverPath;
let EDRSilence = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType == "ProcessTerminated"
    | where FileName has_any (
        "MsMpEng.exe", "csagent.exe", "SentinelAgent.exe",
        "SentinelOne.exe", "cb.exe", "CylanceSvc.exe",
        "mfefire.exe", "bdagent.exe", "avgnt.exe", "ekrn.exe"
    )
    | project SilenceTime=Timestamp, DeviceName, EDRProcess=FileName;
DriverLoadEvents
| join kind=inner EDRSilence on DeviceName
| where SilenceTime between (DriverLoadTime .. (DriverLoadTime + 10m))
| project DriverLoadTime, SilenceTime, DeviceName, DriverPath, EDRProcess
| extend KillChainGap = datetime_diff('second', SilenceTime, DriverLoadTime)
| extend Confidence = "HIGH — driver load immediately preceded EDR silence"
| sort by DriverLoadTime desc


// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// Query 2: Security service stops via sc.exe — rapid succession pattern
// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FileName in ("sc.exe", "net.exe", "net1.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine has "stop"
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (
    "WinDefend", "Sense", "SentinelAgent", "CSFalconService",
    "carbonblack", "CylanceSvc", "McAfeeFramework", "mfemms",
    "bdagent", "wdnissvc", "wscsvc", "MBAMService"
)
| summarize
    ServiceStopCount = count(),
    Services = make_set(ProcessCommandLine),
    FirstStop = min(Timestamp),
    LastStop = max(Timestamp)
    by DeviceName, bin(Timestamp, 10m), AccountName
| where ServiceStopCount >= 2
| extend Duration = datetime_diff('second', LastStop, FirstStop)
| project FirstStop, DeviceName, AccountName, ServiceStopCount, Duration, Services
| extend Confidence = "HIGH — multiple security services stopped in rapid succession"
| sort by FirstStop desc


// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// Query 3: LSASS access following driver load (credential harvest after EDR kill)
// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
let SuspiciousDriverLoads = DeviceEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType == "DriverLoad"
    | extend DriverPath = tostring(AdditionalFields.DriverPath)
    | where DriverPath has_any (
        "RTCore64", "gdrv", "DBUtil_2_3", "LenovoHECI",
        "MsIo64", "AsIO64", "InpoutX64"
    )
    | project DriverLoadTime=Timestamp, DeviceName, DriverPath;
let LsassAccess = DeviceEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType == "OpenProcessApiCall"
    | where FileName == "lsass.exe"
    | where ProcessTokenElevationType == "TokenElevationTypeFull"
    | project LsassTime=Timestamp, DeviceName, AccessingProcess=InitiatingProcessFileName,
              AccessingCmd=InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
SuspiciousDriverLoads
| join kind=inner LsassAccess on DeviceName
| where LsassTime between (DriverLoadTime .. (DriverLoadTime + 15m))
| project DriverLoadTime, LsassTime, DeviceName, DriverPath,
          AccessingProcess, AccessingCmd
| extend KillChainStage = "driver_load → lsass_access (EDR likely killed)"
| sort by DriverLoadTime desc


// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// Query 4: EDR cloud heartbeat gap detection
// Devices where the EDR sensor has not reported for > 5 minutes during business hours
// Adjust the threshold and time window to your environment's normal beacon interval
// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DeviceEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(24h)
| summarize LastSeen = max(Timestamp) by DeviceName
| where LastSeen < ago(5m)
| join kind=inner (
    DeviceEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(48h)
    | summarize EarlierSeen = max(Timestamp) by DeviceName
    | where EarlierSeen between (ago(48h) .. ago(25h))
) on DeviceName
| project DeviceName, LastSeen, EarlierSeen
| extend SilenceDuration = datetime_diff('minute', now(), LastSeen)
| where SilenceDuration > 5
| extend Alert = "EDR sensor silence — potential kill or system offline"
| sort by SilenceDuration desc


// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// Query 5: PPL bypass indicator — high-access process open on EDR process
// A PROCESS_TERMINATE access succeeding against a PPL process indicates PPL was stripped
// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DeviceEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ActionType == "OpenProcessApiCall"
| where FileName has_any (
    "MsMpEng.exe", "csagent.exe", "SentinelAgent.exe",
    "cb.exe", "bdagent.exe"
)
| where GrantedAccess has_any ("0x1f0fff", "0x001", "0x0020")
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in ("services.exe", "smss.exe", "wininit.exe")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, FileName, GrantedAccess,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          AccountName
| extend Interpretation = "PPL bypass suspected — access mask inconsistent with PPL protection"
| sort by Timestamp desc


// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// Query 6: Driver loaded from unexpected path (anomaly — not system32\drivers)
// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DeviceEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ActionType == "DriverLoad"
| extend DriverPath = tostring(AdditionalFields.DriverPath)
| where DriverPath !startswith @"C:\Windows\System32\drivers\"
      and DriverPath !startswith @"C:\Windows\SysWOW64\drivers\"
      and DriverPath !startswith @"C:\Program Files\"
      and DriverPath !startswith @"C:\Program Files (x86)\"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, DriverPath,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          AccountName
| extend Anomaly = "Driver loaded from unexpected path — common in BYOVD dropper"
| sort by Timestamp desc

title: Dmz Egress Blocks
description: KQL query collected from public GitHub repositories.

RULE-32 | AD Security: Accessibility Feature / IFEO Backdoor ============================================================ Description: Detects persistence/privilege escalation via Image File Execution Options (IFEO) debugger hijacking of Windows accessibility binaries (Sticky Keys, Utilman, Narrator, On-Screen Keyboard, Magnifier, Display Switch, ATBroker). Attackers (e.g., Sticky-Keys-Slayer) replace or redirect sethc.exe/utilman.exe to cmd.exe, allowing SYSTEM-level shell access from the Windows logon screen via the accessibility shortcut keys — no credentials required.
content:
// Source: https://github.com/davidalonsod/Dalonso-Security-Repo
// Collected: 2026-05-10
// KQL Hunter - https://github.com/YOUR_USER/kql-hunter

let AccessibilityBinaries = dynamic([
    "sethc.exe",        // Sticky Keys (Shift x5)
    "utilman.exe",      // Utility Manager (Win+U)
    "osk.exe",          // On-Screen Keyboard
    "magnify.exe",      // Magnifier
    "narrator.exe",     // Narrator
    "displayswitch.exe",// Display Switch (Win+P)
    "atbroker.exe"      // Assistive Technology Broker
]);
let SuspiciousDebuggers = dynamic([
    "cmd.exe", "powershell.exe", "powershell_ise.exe",
    "pwsh.exe", "mshta.exe", "wscript.exe", "cscript.exe",
    "rundll32.exe", "regsvr32.exe", "msiexec.exe",
    "net.exe", "whoami.exe"
]);
// --- Vector 1: IFEO registry write ---
let IFEORegistryWrites = SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(10m)
| where EventID == 4657
| where ObjectName has_any (
    @"SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Image File Execution Options\sethc.exe",
    @"SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Image File Execution Options\utilman.exe",
    @"SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Image File Execution Options\osk.exe",
    @"SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Image File Execution Options\magnify.exe",
    @"SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Image File Execution Options\narrator.exe",
    @"SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Image File Execution Options\displayswitch.exe",
    @"SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Image File Execution Options\atbroker.exe"
)
| where ObjectValueName =~ "Debugger" or ObjectValueName =~ "GlobalFlag"
| extend
    Actor              = tolower(strcat(SubjectDomainName, "\\", SubjectUserName)),
    AffectedHost       = Computer,
    TargetKey          = ObjectName,
    ValueName          = ObjectValueName,
    NewValue           = NewValue,
    OldValue           = OldValue,
    DetectionVector    = "IFEO_RegistryWrite",
    MITRE_Technique    = "T1546.008"
| project
    TimeGenerated, AffectedHost, Actor,
    TargetKey, ValueName, OldValue, NewValue,
    DetectionVector, MITRE_Technique;
// --- Vector 2: Accessibility binary spawns shell (EventID 4688) ---
let AccessibilityShellSpawn = SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(10m)
| where EventID == 4688
| extend
    ParentProcess = tolower(tostring(extract(@"([^\\]+)$", 1, ParentProcessName))),
    ChildProcess  = tolower(tostring(extract(@"([^\\]+)$", 1, NewProcessName)))
| where ParentProcess in~ (AccessibilityBinaries)
| where ChildProcess in~ (SuspiciousDebuggers)
| extend
    Actor              = tolower(strcat(SubjectDomainName, "\\", SubjectUserName)),
    AffectedHost       = Computer,
    TargetKey          = strcat("_AccessibilityParent:", ParentProcess),
    ValueName          = "ProcessSpawn",
    OldValue           = ParentProcess,
    NewValue           = strcat(NewProcessName, " ", CommandLine),
    DetectionVector    = "AccessibilityBinary_ShellSpawn",
    MITRE_Technique    = "T1546.008"
| project
    TimeGenerated, AffectedHost, Actor,
    TargetKey, ValueName, OldValue, NewValue,
    DetectionVector, MITRE_Technique;
// --- Vector 3: DeviceProcessEvents (MDE) - accessibility binary parent ---
let MDEAccessibilitySpawn = DeviceProcessEvents
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(10m)
| extend
    ParentBin = tolower(tostring(extract(@"([^\\]+)$", 1, InitiatingProcessFileName))),
    ChildBin  = tolower(FileName)
| where ParentBin in~ (AccessibilityBinaries)
| where ChildBin in~ (SuspiciousDebuggers)
| extend
    Actor              = tolower(strcat(InitiatingProcessAccountDomain, "\\", InitiatingProcessAccountName)),
    AffectedHost       = DeviceName,
    TargetKey          = strcat("_AccessibilityParent:", ParentBin),
    ValueName          = "ProcessSpawn",
    OldValue           = InitiatingProcessFileName,
    NewValue           = strcat(FolderPath, "\\", FileName, " | ", ProcessCommandLine),
    DetectionVector    = "MDE_AccessibilitySpawn",
    MITRE_Technique    = "T1546.008"
| project
    TimeGenerated, AffectedHost, Actor,
    TargetKey, ValueName, OldValue, NewValue,
    DetectionVector, MITRE_Technique;
// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// UNION all vectors and finalize
// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
union IFEORegistryWrites, AccessibilityShellSpawn, MDEAccessibilitySpawn
| summarize
    FirstDetected   = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastDetected    = max(TimeGenerated),
    EventCount      = count(),
    Vectors         = make_set(DetectionVector),
    NewValues       = make_set(NewValue),
    OldValues       = make_set(OldValue),
    AffectedKeys    = make_set(TargetKey)
    by AffectedHost, Actor, MITRE_Technique
| extend
    Severity = iff(
        Vectors has "IFEO_RegistryWrite" and (Vectors has "AccessibilitySpawn" or Vectors has "MDE"),
        "Critical",   // Both registry write AND spawn observed — confirmed exploitation
        "High"        // Either vector alone is already high-confidence
    ),
    AlertName = strcat("Accessibility Feature Backdoor (IFEO): ", Actor, " on ", AffectedHost)
| project-reorder
    FirstDetected, LastDetected, Severity, AlertName,
    AffectedHost, Actor,
    Vectors, AffectedKeys, NewValues, OldValues,
    EventCount, MITRE_Technique

title: Name: Multi-Host Admin Logon (Privilege Escalation Sweep)
description: MITRE: T1078.002 - Lateral Movement / Valid Accounts: Domain Accounts Severity: High Description: Detects a single account being assigned special privileges (admin logon) across multiple hosts in a short period, indicating potential lateral movement or domain compromise. Query Frequency: 1h Query Period: 1h Trigger: gt 0
content:
// Source: https://github.com/n1ops/sentinel-detection-lab
// Collected: 2026-05-10
// KQL Hunter - https://github.com/YOUR_USER/kql-hunter

let timeframe = 1h;
let host_threshold = 3;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(timeframe)
| where EventID == 4672  // Special privileges assigned to new logon
| where Account !endswith "$"  // Exclude machine accounts
| where Account !in~ ("NT AUTHORITY\\SYSTEM", "NT AUTHORITY\\LOCAL SERVICE", "NT AUTHORITY\\NETWORK SERVICE")
| extend
    AccountDomain = extract("^(.+)\\\\", 1, Account),
    AccountName = extract("\\\\(.+)$", 1, Account)
| summarize
    DistinctHosts = dcount(Computer),
    TargetHosts = make_set(Computer, 50),
    TotalLogons = count(),
    FirstLogon = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastLogon = max(TimeGenerated),
    PrivilegeList = make_set(PrivilegeList, 10),
    LogonTypes = make_set(LogonType, 10)
    by Account, AccountDomain, AccountName
| where DistinctHosts > host_threshold
| extend
    SweepDurationMinutes = datetime_diff('minute', LastLogon, FirstLogon),
    HostsPerMinute = iff(
        datetime_diff('minute', LastLogon, FirstLogon) > 0,
        round(toreal(DistinctHosts) / toreal(datetime_diff('minute', LastLogon, FirstLogon)), 2),
        toreal(DistinctHosts)
    ),
    Entity_Account = Account,
    Entity_IP = IpAddress
| project
    FirstLogon,
    LastLogon,
    Account,
    AccountDomain,
    AccountName,
    DistinctHosts,
    TargetHosts,
    TotalLogons,
    SweepDurationMinutes,
    HostsPerMinute,
    LogonTypes,
    Entity_Account,
    Entity_IP
| order by DistinctHosts desc

title: Credit: https://learnsentinel.blog/2022/06/21/kql-lessons-learnt-from-365daysofkql/
description: Credit: https://x.com/reprise_99?mx=2
content:
// Source: https://github.com/ChrFrohn/KQL
// Collected: 2026-05-10
// KQL Hunter - https://github.com/YOUR_USER/kql-hunter

let aaduseradded=
    AuditLogs
    | where OperationName == "Add member to group"
    | extend Actor = tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy.user)).userPrincipalName)
    | extend Target = tostring(TargetResources[0].userPrincipalName)
    | extend GroupName = tostring(parse_json(tostring(parse_json(tostring(TargetResources[0].modifiedProperties))[1].newValue)))
    | extend GroupID = tostring(parse_json(tostring(parse_json(tostring(TargetResources[0].modifiedProperties))[0].newValue)))
    | where isnotempty(Actor) and isnotempty(Target)
    | extend Environment = strcat("Azure Active Directory")
    | extend Action = strcat("Add")
    | project TimeGenerated, Action, Actor, Target, GroupName, GroupID, Environment;
let aaduserremoved=
    AuditLogs
    | where OperationName == "Remove member from group"
    | extend Actor = tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy.user)).userPrincipalName)
    | extend GroupName = tostring(parse_json(tostring(parse_json(tostring(TargetResources[0].modifiedProperties))[1].oldValue)))
    | extend GroupID = tostring(parse_json(tostring(parse_json(tostring(TargetResources[0].modifiedProperties))[0].oldValue)))
    | extend Target = tostring(TargetResources[0].userPrincipalName)
    | where isnotempty(Actor) and isnotempty(Target)
    | extend Action = strcat("Remove")
    | extend Environment = strcat("Azure Active Directory")
    | project TimeGenerated, Action, Actor, Target, GroupName, GroupID, Environment;
let adchanges=
    SecurityEvent
    | project TimeGenerated, EventID, AccountType, MemberName, SubjectUserName, TargetUserName,TargetSid
    | where AccountType == "User"
    | where EventID in (4728, 4729, 4732, 4733, 4756, 4757)
    | parse MemberName with * 'CN=' Target ',OU=' *
    | extend Action = case(EventID in ("4728", "4756", "4732"), strcat("Add"),
        EventID in ("4729", "4757", "4733"), strcat("Remove"), "unknown")
    | extend Environment = strcat("Active Directory")
    | project
        TimeGenerated,
        Action,
        Actor=SubjectUserName,
        Target,
        GroupName=TargetUserName,
        GroupID =TargetSid,
        Environment;
union aaduseradded, aaduserremoved, adchanges

content:
// Source: https://github.com/le0li9ht/Microsoft-Sentinel-Queries
// Collected: 2026-05-10
// KQL Hunter - https://github.com/YOUR_USER/kql-hunter

let Threshold=1;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >ago(1d)
| where EventID in ("4723","4724")
| extend SubjectUserSid = tostring(SubjectUserSid)
| extend SubjectAccount = strcat(tostring(SubjectDomainName), "\\", tostring(SubjectUserName))
| extend AccountType = case(Account endswith "$" or SubjectUserSid in ("S-1-5-18", "S-1-5-19", "S-1-5-20"), "Machine", isempty(SubjectUserSid), "", "User")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, AccountType, SubjectAccount, TargetUserName, EventID
| summarize
        StartTimeUtc = min(TimeGenerated),
        EndTimeUtc = max(TimeGenerated),
        ComputerList = make_set(Computer, 25),
        AccountType = make_set(AccountType, 25),
        TargetUserList = make_set(TargetUserName, 25),
        EventIDs=make_set(EventID,3),
        TotalTargets=count() by  SubjectAccount
| where TotalTargets > Threshold
| extend ActivityDescription="SubjectAccount did password reset for multiple users from TargetuserList"
| project-reorder StartTimeUtc,EndTimeUtc, SubjectAccount, ComputerList, TargetUserList, AccountType,EventIDs, TotalTargets, ActivityDescription

title: Parsed Events Ids
description: Parsed Events Ids
content:
// Source: https://github.com/noakup/AzMonLogsAgent
// Collected: 2026-05-10
// KQL Hunter - https://github.com/YOUR_USER/kql-hunter

let parser = (disabled: bool = false) {
  // Parsed Events Ids
  let ParsedEventIds = dynamic([4698, 4699, 4700, 4701, 4702, 4929, 5025, 5027, 5028, 5029, 5030, 5034, 5035, 5037, 7035, 7036, 7040, 7045, 2009, 5136]);
  // Eventlog Event Ids
  let EventlogEventIds = dynamic([1102]);
  // Scheduled Task Event Ids
  let ScheduledTaskEventIds = dynamic([4698, 4699, 4700, 4701, 4702]);
  // Active Directory Replica Source Naming Context Event Ids
  let ActiveDirectoryReplicaIds = dynamic([4929]);
  // Firewall Event Ids
  let FirewallEventIds = dynamic([5025, 5027, 5028, 5029, 5030, 5034, 5035, 5037]);
  // Service Event Ids
  let ServiceEventIds = dynamic([7035, 7036, 7040, 7045, 2009]); 
  // Directory Service Object Ids
  let DirectoryServiceIds = dynamic([5136]);
  // Clear Audit Log Event
  let AuditLogClearedEventID = dynamic([1102]); 
  // EventID Lookup
  let EventIDLookup = datatable(
  EventID: int,
  Operation: string,
  EventType: string,
  Object: string,
  ObjectType: string,
  EventResult: string
  )
          [   
      1102, "Delete Logs", "Delete", "Security Logs", "Event Log", "Success",
      4698, "Create Scheduled Task", "Create", "", "Scheduled Task", "Success",
      4699, "Delete Scheduled Task", "Delete", "", "Scheduled Task", "Success",
      4700, "Enable Scheduled Task", "Enable", "", "Scheduled Task", "Success",
      4701, "Disable Scheduled Task ", "Disable", "", "Scheduled Task", "Success",
      4702, "Update Scheduled Task", "Set", "", "Scheduled Task", "Success",
      4929, "Remove Active Directory Replica Source Naming Context", "Delete", "", "Other", "Success",
      5025, "Stop Firewall Service", "Disable", "Firewall Service", "Service", "Success",
      5027, "Retrieve the Security Policy From The Local Storage", "Read", "Firewall Service", "Service", "Failure",
      5028, "Parse the new Security Policy", "Set", "Firewall Service", "Service", "Failure",
      5029, "Initialize the Firewall Driver", "Initialize", "Firewall Service", "Service", "Failure",
      5030, "Start the Firewall Service", "Start", "Firewall Service", "Service", "Failure",
      5034, "Stop Firewall Driver", "Stop", "Firewall Driver", "Driver", "Failure",
      5035, "Start Firewall Driver", "Start", "Firewall Driver", "Driver", "Failure",
      5037, "Terminating Firewall Driver", "Terminate", "Firewall Driver", "Driver", "Failure",
      7035, "Start Control Sent", "Execute", "Service", "Service", "Success",
      7036, "Enter Stop State", "Stop", "Service", "Service", "Success",
      7040, "Changed Service Settings", "Set", "Service", "Service", "Success",
      7045, "Install Service", "Install", "Service", "Service", "Success",
      2009, "Load Group Policy", "Other", "Service", "Service", "Failure",
      5136, "Modified Directory Services Object", "Set", "", "Directory Service Object", "Success"
  ];
      let ParsedEvents =
          union
              (
              union
                  (
                  // SecurityEvents
                  SecurityEvent
                  | where not(disabled)
                  | where EventID in(ParsedEventIds)
                  | project EventID, EventData, _ResourceId, TimeGenerated, Computer, Type, _ItemId
                  ),
                  (
                  // Event
                  Event
                  | where not(disabled)
                  | where EventID in(ParsedEventIds)
                  | project EventID, EventData, _ResourceId, TimeGenerated, Computer, Type, _ItemId
                  )
              | parse-kv EventData as 
                  (
                  SubjectUserSid: string,
                  SubjectUserName: string,
                  SubjectDomainName: string,
                  SubjectLogonId: string,
                  TaskName: string,
                  TaskContent: string,
                  TaskContentNew: string,
                  ClientProcessId: string,
                  DestinationDRA: string,
                  SourceDRA: string,
                  SourceAddr: string,
                  ObjectDN: string,
                  AttributeValue: string
                  ) 
                  with (regex=@'<Data Name="(\w+)">{?([^<]*?)}?</Data>')
              | project-away EventData
              ),
              // WindowsEvents
              (
              WindowsEvent
              | where not(disabled)
              | where EventID in(ParsedEventIds)
              | project EventID, EventData, _ResourceId, TimeGenerated, Computer, Type, _ItemId
              | extend
                  SubjectUserSid = tostring(EventData.SubjectUserSid),
                  SubjectUserName = tostring(EventData.SubjectUserName),
                  SubjectDomainName = tostring(EventData.SubjectDomainName),
                  SubjectLogonId = tostring(EventData.SubjectLogonId),
                  TaskName = tostring(EventData.TaskName),
                  TaskContent = tostring(EventData.TaskContent),
                  TaskContentNew = tostring(EventData.TaskContentNew),
                  ClientProcessId = tostring(EventData.ClientProcessId),
                  DestinationDRA = tostring(EventData.DestinationDRA),
                  SourceDRA = tostring(EventData.SourceDRA),
                  SourceAddr = tostring(EventData.SourceAddr),
                  ObjectDN = tostring(EventData.ObjectDN),
                  AttributeValue = tostring(EventData.AttributeValue)
              | project-away EventData
              ),
              //Section for SecurityEvent(1102)
              (
              SecurityEvent
              | where not(disabled)
              | where EventID in (AuditLogClearedEventID) and EventSourceName == "Microsoft-Windows-Eventlog"
              | project EventID, EventData, _ResourceId, TimeGenerated, Computer, Type, _ItemId
              | extend Parsed_EventData = parse_xml(EventData)
              | extend
                  SubjectUserSid = tostring(Parsed_EventData.UserData.LogFileCleared.SubjectUserSid),
                  SubjectUserName = tostring(Parsed_EventData.UserData.LogFileCleared.SubjectUserName),
                  SubjectDomainName = tostring(Parsed_EventData.UserData.LogFileCleared.SubjectDomainName),
                  SubjectLogonId = tostring(Parsed_EventData.UserData.LogFileCleared.SubjectLogonId)
              | project-away EventData, Parsed_EventData
              ),
              // Section for WindowsEvent(1102)
              (
              WindowsEvent
              | where not(disabled)
              | where EventID in (AuditLogClearedEventID) and Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Eventlog"
              | project EventID, EventData, _ResourceId, TimeGenerated, Computer, Type, _ItemId
              | extend
                  SubjectUserSid = tostring(EventData.SubjectUserSid),
                  SubjectUserName = tostring(EventData.SubjectUserName),
                  SubjectDomainName = tostring(EventData.SubjectDomainName),
                  SubjectLogonId = tostring(EventData.SubjectLogonId)
              | project-away EventData
              )
          | lookup EventIDLookup on EventID
      ;
      // Parse EventLog
      let EventLog = ParsedEvents
          | where EventID in(EventlogEventIds)
          | project-away Task*, *DRA, SourceAddr, ObjectDN, AttributeValue;
      // Parse Scheduled Task
      let ScheduledTask = ParsedEvents
          | where EventID in(ScheduledTaskEventIds)
          | extend 
              Object = TaskName,
              NewValue = coalesce(
              TaskContent,
              TaskContentNew
          )
          | extend 
              Value = NewValue
          | project-away Task*, *DRA, SourceAddr, ObjectDN, AttributeValue
      ;
      // Parse ADR
      let ActiveDirectoryReplica = ParsedEvents
          | where EventID in(ActiveDirectoryReplicaIds)
          | extend 
              NewValue = SourceDRA,
              OldValue = DestinationDRA,
              SrcFQDN = SourceAddr
          | extend 
              Value = NewValue,
              Object = OldValue
          | project-away Task*, *DRA, SourceAddr, ObjectDN, AttributeValue
      ;
      // Parse WindowsFirewall
      let WindowsFirewall = ParsedEvents
          | where EventID in(FirewallEventIds)
          | project-away Task*, *DRA, SourceAddr, ObjectDN, AttributeValue
      ;
      // Parse ServiceEvent
      let ServiceEvent = ParsedEvents
          | where EventID in(ServiceEventIds)
          | project-away Task*, *DRA, SourceAddr, ObjectDN, AttributeValue
      ;
      // Parse DirectoryService
      let DirectoryService = ParsedEvents
          | where EventID in(DirectoryServiceIds)
          | extend 
              Object = ObjectDN
          | project-rename 
              NewValue = AttributeValue
          | extend
              Value = NewValue
          | project-away Task*, *DRA, SourceAddr, ObjectDN
      ;
      // Union Events
      union
          EventLog,
          ScheduledTask,
          ActiveDirectoryReplica,
          WindowsFirewall,
          ServiceEvent,
          DirectoryService
      | invoke _ASIM_ResolveDvcFQDN("Computer")
      | project-rename 
          ActorUserId = SubjectUserSid,
          ActorSessionId = SubjectLogonId,
          DvcId = _ResourceId,
          ActingAppId = ClientProcessId,
          EventUid = _ItemId
      | extend
          EventCount = int(1),
          EventStartTime = TimeGenerated, 
          EventEndTime= TimeGenerated,
          EventProduct = 'Security Events',
          EventVendor = 'Microsoft',
          EventSchemaVersion = '0.1.0',
          EventSchema = 'AuditEvent',
          EventOriginalType = tostring(EventID),
          DvcIdType = iff (DvcId == "", "", "AzureResourceID"),
          ActorUsername = iff (SubjectDomainName == "", SubjectUserName, strcat (SubjectDomainName, '\\', SubjectUserName)),
          ActorUsernameType = iff (SubjectDomainName == "", 'Simple', 'Windows'),
          ActorUserIdType = iff (ActorUserId == "", "", "SID"),
          ActingAppType = "Process"
      | extend
          User = ActorUsername,
          Dvc = DvcFQDN
      | project-away Subject*, EventID, Computer
  };
  parser (disabled=disabled)

title: Persistence Indicators (Scheduled Tasks, Services, Run Keys)
description: T1053.005 (Scheduled Task), T1547.001 (Run Keys), Service Install (4697/7045/Sysmon 6)
content:
// Source: https://github.com/frances-saunders/cybersecurity-portfolio
// Collected: 2026-05-10
// KQL Hunter - https://github.com/YOUR_USER/kql-hunter

let timeWindow = 24h;
let suspiciousPaths = dynamic(["\\Users\\","\\AppData\\","\\Temp\\","\\ProgramData\\","\\Windows\\Tasks\\"]);
union isfuzzy=true
(
  SecurityEvent
  | where TimeGenerated > ago(timeWindow)
  | where EventID in (4698, 106, 140) // Task creation (varies by provider)
  | extend Detail = coalesce(CommandLine, EventData, NewProcessName)
  | where Detail has_any (suspiciousPaths)
  | extend Technique="T1053.005", Tactic="Persistence"
),
(
  SecurityEvent
  | where TimeGenerated > ago(timeWindow)
  | where EventID in (4697) // Service install
  | extend ServiceName = tostring(EventData["ServiceName"])
  | extend ImagePath   = tostring(EventData["ImagePath"])
  | where ImagePath has_any (suspiciousPaths)
  | extend Technique="Service Install", Tactic="Persistence"
),
(
  Sysmon
  | where TimeGenerated > ago(timeWindow)
  | where EventID in (13) // Registry value set
  | extend TargetObject= tostring(EventData["TargetObject"])
  | extend Details= tostring(EventData["Details"])
  | where TargetObject matches regex @"\\Software\\(Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run|RunOnce)"
  | extend Technique="T1547.001", Tactic="Persistence"
)
| extend Host = coalesce(Computer, tostring(ComputerName))
| extend Account = coalesce(SubjectUserName, tostring(User))
| extend Severity=iff(Details has ".exe", "High","Medium")
| project TimeGenerated, Host, Account, Technique, Tactic, ServiceName, ImagePath, TargetObject, Details
| order by TimeGenerated desc

title: Securityevent Possible Unusual Remote Session In A Domain Controller
description: KQL query collected from public GitHub repositories.
content:
// Source: https://github.com/ep3p/Sentinel_KQL
// Collected: 2026-05-10
// KQL Hunter - https://github.com/YOUR_USER/kql-hunter

let query_frequency = 1h;
let query_period = 14d;
let _DomainControllers = toscalar(
    _GetWatchlist("Service-PrivateCorporateServices")
    | where Service == "DomainController"
    | summarize make_list(HostName)
);
let _ExpectedRemoteSessionAccounts = toscalar(
    _GetWatchlist("Activity-ExpectedSignificantActivity")
    | where Activity == "DomainControllerRemoteSession"
    | summarize make_list(ActorPrincipalName)
);
let _ExpectedIPs = toscalar(
    _GetWatchlist("Service-PrivateCorporateServices")
    | where Notes has "[PAM]"
    | summarize make_list(IPAddress)
);
union
    (
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(query_period)
    | where EventID == 4673 and Computer has_any (_DomainControllers)// and Process has "svchost.exe"
    | summarize
        Count = count(),
        arg_min(TimeGenerated, *)
        by Computer, Account, Process
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(query_frequency)
    ),
    (
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(query_period)
    | where EventID == 4674 and Computer has_any (_DomainControllers)
    | where (ObjectType has "Key" and (
            ObjectName has_all (@"\REGISTRY\USER\", @"_Classes\Local Settings\MuiCache\")
            or ObjectName has_all (@"\REGISTRY\USER\", @"\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\CloudStore")
            or ObjectName has @"\REGISTRY\MACHINE\SYSTEM\ControlSet001\Services\WinSock2\Parameters"))
        or ObjectName has_all (@"\BaseNamedObjects\", @"WilStaging")
    | summarize
        Count = count(),
        ObjectNames = array_sort_asc(make_set(ObjectName, 50)),
        arg_min(TimeGenerated, *)
        by Computer, Account, ObjectType
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(query_frequency)
    ),
    (
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(query_period)
    | where EventID == 4688 and Computer has_any (_DomainControllers) and not(TargetAccount has_any (@"Window Manager\DWM-", @"Font Driver Host\UMFD-"))// and NewProcessName has "wsmprovhost.exe"
    | summarize
        Count = count(),
        NewProcessNames = array_sort_asc(make_set(NewProcessName, 50)),
        arg_min(TimeGenerated, *)
        by Computer, Account, TargetAccount
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(query_frequency)
    )
| join kind=leftouter (
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(query_period)
    | where Computer has_any (_DomainControllers) and EventID == 4624 and not(LogonType in (3))
    | project
        Logon_TimeGenerated = TimeGenerated,
        Computer,
        Account,
        Logon_IpAddress = IpAddress,
        TargetLogonId
    ) on Computer, Account, $left.SubjectLogonId == $right.TargetLogonId
| project-away *1
| where not(Account in (_ExpectedRemoteSessionAccounts) and Logon_IpAddress in (_ExpectedIPs))
| where not(EventID == 4688 and TargetAccount in (_ExpectedRemoteSessionAccounts))
| sort by Activity asc, Computer asc, Account asc, TargetAccount asc, TimeGenerated asc
| project
    TimeGenerated,
    Computer,
    AccountType,
    Account,
    TargetAccount,
    Activity,
    Count,
    ObjectType,
    ObjectNames,
    NewProcessNames,
    AccessMask,
    ProcessName,
    PrivilegeList,
    SubjectLogonId,
    TargetLogonId,
    Logon_TimeGenerated,
    Logon_IpAddress,
    EventData

title: Data connector required for this query - Windows Security Events via AMA or Security Events via Legacy Agent

title: Risky Sign-In From Unknown Device and Rarely Used IP
description: Detects risky sign-in attempts from unmanaged/non-onboarded devices or IP addresses rarely used by the user. Classifies risk as High (new IP) or Medium (IP seen fewer than 3 distinct days in 29 days).
author: Benjamin Zulliger
content:
// Title: Risky Sign-In From Unknown Device and Rarely Used IP
// Author: Benjamin Zulliger
// Source: https://www.kqlsearch.com
// MITRE: T1078.004
// KQL Library - https://github.com/sivolko/kql-hunter
let CorporateIPRange = "0.0.0.0/16";
let LookbackHistory = 29d;
let TodayStart = startofday(now());
let AffectedUsers = materialize(
    BehaviorAnalytics
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(4h)
    | where not(ipv4_is_in_range(SourceIPAddress, CorporateIPRange))
    | where isnotempty(UserPrincipalName)
    | where ActivityType != "FailedLogOn"
    | where ActivityInsights.FirstTimeUserConnectedFromDevice == true
    | join kind=inner (IdentityInfo | summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated,*) by AccountUpn | project AccountUpn, TenantMembershipType) on $left.UserPrincipalName == $right.AccountUpn
    | where TenantMembershipType == "Member"
    | distinct UserPrincipalName);
let HistoricalIPs = EntraIdSignInEvents
    | where TimeGenerated between ((TodayStart - LookbackHistory) .. (TodayStart - 1ms))
    | where AccountUpn in (AffectedUsers) | where ErrorCode == 0
    | summarize DistinctDaysSeenFromIP = dcount(bin(TimeGenerated, 1d)) by AccountUpn, IPAddress
    | project UserPrincipalName = AccountUpn, SourceIPAddress = IPAddress, DistinctDaysSeenFromIP;
BehaviorAnalytics
| where TimeGenerated > ago(4h)
| where not(ipv4_is_in_range(SourceIPAddress, CorporateIPRange))
| where isnotempty(UserPrincipalName) | where ActivityType != "FailedLogOn"
| where ActivityInsights.FirstTimeUserConnectedFromDevice == true
| join kind=inner hint.strategy=broadcast (IdentityInfo | summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated,*) by AccountUpn | project AccountUpn, TenantMembershipType) on $left.UserPrincipalName == $right.AccountUpn
| where TenantMembershipType == "Member"
| extend SourceDeviceLower = tolower(SourceDevice)
| join kind=leftouter (DeviceInfo | extend DeviceNameLower = tolower(DeviceName) | project DeviceNameLower, DeviceName, DeviceId, OnboardingStatus) on $left.SourceDeviceLower == $right.DeviceNameLower
| where OnboardingStatus != "Onboarded" or isempty(OnboardingStatus)
| join kind=leftouter hint.strategy=broadcast (HistoricalIPs) on UserPrincipalName, SourceIPAddress
| extend IPRiskClassification = case(isempty(DistinctDaysSeenFromIP), "High Risk - New IP", DistinctDaysSeenFromIP < 3, "Medium Risk - Infrequent IP", "Low Risk")
| where IPRiskClassification != "Low Risk"
| summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by UserPrincipalName
| where isempty(DeviceName) | where DistinctDaysSeenFromIP < 2

author: Benjamin Zulliger

title: Copilot Jailbreak Detected
description: Retrieves Copilot interaction events where a jailbreak attempt was detected. Maps to MITRE ATLAS AML.T0054.
author: Alex Verboon

title: Git Abuse High Fidelity — Repository Tampering
description: High-confidence detection correlating Git identity changes, commit amendments, force pushes, and time manipulation on the same device within 30 minutes.
author: Ali Hussein

title: Privileged RDP Session Source Mismatch
description: Detects RDP logons with privileged accounts from devices where the account owner has not recently logged in, indicating lateral movement or credential theft.
author: Benjamin Zulliger

title: Audit B2B Guest Devices Trust Type
description: Audits B2B guest sign-ins grouped by device trust type to identify unmanaged or unknown devices used by external guest accounts.
author: Jay Kerai
content:
// Title: Audit B2B Guest Devices Trust Type
// Author: Jay Kerai
// Source: https://www.kqlsearch.com
// KQL Library - https://github.com/sivolko/kql-hunter
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where UserType == "Guest"
| extend TrustType = tostring(DeviceDetail.trustType)
| extend DeviceOS = tostring(DeviceDetail.operatingSystem)
| extend IsManaged = tostring(DeviceDetail.isManaged)
| summarize SignInCount = count(), UniqueIPs = dcount(IPAddress)
    by UserPrincipalName, TrustType, DeviceOS, IsManaged, ResultType
| extend DeviceRisk = case(
    TrustType == "", "Unregistered Device",
    TrustType == "AzureAD", "Cloud Joined",
    TrustType == "Hybrid", "Hybrid Joined",
    "Other")
| order by SignInCount desc

title: SharePoint Phishing Click + Verification Code + Suspicious Login
description: Multi-stage detection correlating a SharePoint phishing URL click, a one-time verification code email, and a subsequent suspicious Entra ID sign-in.
author: Benjamin Zulliger

title: Post-Phishing URL Click Suspicious Sign-Ins
description: Correlates Entra ID sign-in events that follow a phishing URL click in email, flagging suspicious logins occurring within 60 minutes of the click.
author: Benjamin Zulliger

title: High-Privilege Takeover: Agent ID Administrator Role Abuse
description: Detects assignment of the Agent ID Administrator role in Entra ID audit logs, which can be abused for high-privilege takeover scenarios.
author: Benjamin Zulliger

title: Session IDs from Multiple OS and User Agents (Token Theft Detection)
description: Detects the same Entra ID session being used from multiple operating systems or user agents, a strong indicator of token theft and session hijacking.
author: Benjamin Zulliger
content:
// Title: Session IDs from Multiple OS and User Agents (Token Theft)
// Author: Benjamin Zulliger
// Source: https://www.kqlsearch.com
// MITRE: T1539 - Steal Web Session Cookie
// KQL Library - https://github.com/sivolko/kql-hunter
EntraIdSignInEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(1d)
| where isnotempty(SessionId) and isnotempty(AccountUpn)
| summarize
    UniqueOSCount = dcount(OSPlatform),
    UniqueUACount = dcount(UserAgent),
    OSList = make_set(OSPlatform),
    UAList = make_set(UserAgent),
    IPList = make_set(IPAddress),
    SignInCount = count()
    by SessionId, AccountUpn
| where UniqueOSCount > 1 or UniqueUACount > 2
| extend Severity = case(UniqueOSCount > 1, "High", UniqueUACount > 2, "Medium", "Low")
| order by UniqueOSCount desc, UniqueUACount desc

title: Multiple Unusual Network Adapter Vendors on Device
description: Identifies devices with multiple unusual or potentially suspicious network adapter vendors, which may indicate rogue hardware or network interception.
author: Jose Sebastian Canos
content:
// Title: Multiple Unusual Network Adapter Vendors
// Author: Jose Sebastian Canos
// Source: https://www.kqlsearch.com
// KQL Library - https://github.com/sivolko/kql-hunter
let KnownVendors = dynamic(["Intel", "Realtek", "Broadcom", "Qualcomm", "Microsoft", "VMware", "Hyper-V"]);
DeviceNetworkInfo
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| mv-expand NetworkAdapters = parse_json(NetworkAdapters)
| extend Vendor = tostring(NetworkAdapters.Vendor)
| where isnotempty(Vendor)
| extend IsKnownVendor = iff(Vendor has_any (KnownVendors), true, false)
| summarize
    UnknownVendors = make_set_if(Vendor, IsKnownVendor == false),
    UnknownVendorCount = dcountif(Vendor, IsKnownVendor == false),
    AllVendors = make_set(Vendor)
    by DeviceId, DeviceName
| join kind=inner (DeviceInfo | where Timestamp > ago(7d) | summarize arg_max(Timestamp,*) by DeviceId | project DeviceId, OSPlatform, PublicIP) on DeviceId
| where UnknownVendorCount >= 2
| order by UnknownVendorCount desc

title: New Tenant Allow/Block List (TABL) Entry
description: Detects new entries added to the Tenant Allow/Block List in Microsoft 365 Defender, which could be used to whitelist malicious senders or URLs.
author: Jay Kerai

title: Intune Operational Logs: Unexpected Device OS Type Modification
description: Detects unexpected changes to device OS type in Intune operational logs, which may indicate device spoofing or policy tampering.
author: Jose Sebastian Canos
content:
// Title: Intune Unexpected Device OS Type Modification
// Author: Jose Sebastian Canos
// Source: https://www.kqlsearch.com
// KQL Library - https://github.com/sivolko/kql-hunter
IntuneOperationalLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where OperationName has_any ("Update", "Modify")
| extend Properties = parse_json(Properties)
| extend DeviceId = tostring(Properties.IntuneDeviceId)
| extend OldOS = tostring(Properties.OldValue)
| extend NewOS = tostring(Properties.NewValue)
| where isnotempty(OldOS) and isnotempty(NewOS)
| where OldOS != NewOS
| where NewOS has_any ("Windows", "iOS", "Android", "macOS")
| project TimeGenerated, DeviceId, OldOS, NewOS, OperationName, Result
| order by TimeGenerated desc

title: Multiple Registered Devices By Account
description: Identifies accounts that have registered an unusual number of devices across Entra ID, Intune, and identity logs — a potential indicator of device registration abuse.
author: Jose Sebastian Canos
content:
// Title: Multiple Registered Devices By Account
// Author: Jose Sebastian Canos
// Source: https://www.kqlsearch.com
// KQL Library - https://github.com/sivolko/kql-hunter
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where OperationName has_any ("Register device", "Add device")
| extend Initiator = tostring(InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName)
| extend DeviceId = tostring(TargetResources[0].id)
| where isnotempty(Initiator)
| summarize DeviceCount = dcount(DeviceId), DeviceIds = make_set(DeviceId) by Initiator, bin(TimeGenerated, 1d)
| where DeviceCount >= 3
| join kind=leftouter (IdentityInfo | summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated,*) by AccountUpn | project AccountUpn, Department, JobTitle) on $left.Initiator == $right.AccountUpn
| order by DeviceCount desc

title: Detect Potential Malicious Emails via InternetMessageId Dates
description: Identifies potentially malicious emails by detecting anomalies in InternetMessageId date components, which can reveal spoofed or manipulated email headers.
author: Sergio Albea

title: Detect Microsoft OTP Emails via InternetMessageId ODSPNOTIFY
description: Identifies Microsoft one-time passcode emails sent as part of SharePoint/OneDrive sharing flows, useful for tracking external sharing activity.
author: Sergio Albea
content:
// Title: Detect Microsoft OTP Emails via InternetMessageId ODSPNOTIFY
// Author: Sergio Albea
// Source: https://www.kqlsearch.com
// KQL Library - https://github.com/sivolko/kql-hunter
EmailEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where InternetMessageId has "odspnotify"
| where Subject has_any ("verification code", "one-time", "passcode", "OTP")
| summarize
    EmailCount = count(),
    Recipients = make_set(RecipientEmailAddress, 10),
    Senders = make_set(SenderFromAddress, 5)
    by bin(Timestamp, 1d)
| order by EmailCount desc

title: Detect Microsoft Shared File Messages via InternetMessageId ODSPNOTIFY
description: Detects Microsoft shared file notification emails by their InternetMessageId pattern, useful for auditing external file sharing.
author: Sergio Albea

title: Audit Logs: Unexpected Device OS Type Modification
description: Detects OS type changes for devices in Entra ID audit logs, which may indicate device record tampering or spoofing.
author: Jose Sebastian Canos
content:
// Title: Audit Logs: Unexpected Device OS Type Modification
// Author: Jose Sebastian Canos
// Source: https://www.kqlsearch.com
// KQL Library - https://github.com/sivolko/kql-hunter
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where OperationName has_any ("Update device", "Add device")
| extend ModifiedProperties = parse_json(TargetResources)[0].modifiedProperties
| mv-expand ModifiedProperties
| where ModifiedProperties.displayName == "DeviceOSType"
| extend OldValue = tostring(ModifiedProperties.oldValue)
| extend NewValue = tostring(ModifiedProperties.newValue)
| where OldValue != NewValue
| extend Actor = tostring(InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName)
| project TimeGenerated, Actor, OldValue, NewValue, OperationName, Result
| order by TimeGenerated desc

title: MDI: AD Group Policy Password Policy
description: Queries Microsoft Defender for Identity events to enumerate AD Group Policy objects related to password policies.
author: Alex Verboon
content:
// Title: MDI AD Group Policy Password Policy
// Author: Alex Verboon
// Source: https://www.kqlsearch.com
// KQL Library - https://github.com/sivolko/kql-hunter
IdentityDirectoryEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where ActionType == "Group Policy Object modified"
| extend GPOName = tostring(AdditionalFields.GPOName)
| extend AttributeChanged = tostring(AdditionalFields.AttributeChanged)
| extend OldValue = tostring(AdditionalFields.OldValue)
| extend NewValue = tostring(AdditionalFields.NewValue)
| where GPOName has_any ("Password", "Default Domain", "Account Policy")
    or AttributeChanged has_any ("minPwdLength", "maxPwdAge", "lockoutThreshold", "pwdHistoryLength")
| project Timestamp, GPOName, AttributeChanged, OldValue, NewValue, AccountName, DeviceName
| order by Timestamp desc

title: Sentinel Watchlists Overview
description: Lists all Microsoft Sentinel watchlists and their entry counts, useful for auditing watchlist coverage and identifying stale or empty watchlists.
author: Alex Verboon

title: Power Platform Customer Lockbox Activity
description: Monitors Power Platform admin activity for Customer Lockbox events, which track Microsoft engineer access requests to customer data.
author: Alex Verboon
content:
// Title: Power Platform Customer Lockbox Activity
// Author: Alex Verboon
// Source: https://www.kqlsearch.com
// KQL Library - https://github.com/sivolko/kql-hunter
PowerPlatformAdminActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(90d)
| where EventOriginalType has_any ("CustomerLockbox", "Lockbox")
| extend RequestId = tostring(parse_json(AdditionalInfo).RequestId)
| extend RequestState = tostring(parse_json(AdditionalInfo).RequestState)
| project TimeGenerated, ActorName, EventOriginalType, RequestId, RequestState, IPAddress
| union (
    CloudAppEvents
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(90d)
    | where ActionType has "CustomerLockbox"
    | project TimeGenerated, AccountDisplayName, ActionType, IPAddress
)
| order by TimeGenerated desc

title: Copilot Agents: Sharing Activity
description: Tracks sharing events for Microsoft Copilot agents across CloudAppEvents and IdentityInfo to audit who is sharing agents and with whom.
author: Alex Verboon

title: Copilot Agent Approval Events
description: Tracks approval events for Copilot agents via OfficeActivity and CloudAppEvents, auditing who approved or rejected agent deployments.
author: Alex Verboon
content:
// Title: Copilot Agent Approval Events
// Author: Alex Verboon
// Source: https://www.kqlsearch.com
// KQL Library - https://github.com/sivolko/kql-hunter
OfficeActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where Operation has_any ("AgentApproved", "AgentRejected", "CopilotAgentApproval")
| extend AgentName = tostring(parse_json(ExtendedProperties)[0].Value)
| project TimeGenerated, UserId, Operation, AgentName, ClientIP, UserAgent
| union (
    CloudAppEvents
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | where ActionType has_any ("AgentApproved", "AgentRejected")
    | extend AgentName = tostring(RawEventData.AgentName)
    | project TimeGenerated, AccountDisplayName, ActionType, AgentName, IPAddress
)
| order by TimeGenerated desc

title: Copilot Agents: Allowed Agent Types
description: Queries CloudAppEvents to enumerate what types of Copilot agents have been allowed or configured in the tenant.
author: Alex Verboon

title: Copilot Agents: User Access
description: Audits which users have been granted access to specific Copilot agents, combining CloudAppEvents with IdentityInfo for enrichment.
author: Alex Verboon

title: Unusual User Agent from Registered Device Bypassing Conditional Access
description: Detects sign-ins from registered devices using unusual user agents that differ from what Conditional Access expects, potentially indicating bypass attempts.
author: Jose Sebastian Canos

title: Defender RedSun: Named Pipe Detection Correlated to AV
description: Detects the RedSun named pipe technique correlated with antivirus detections, indicating possible defense evasion via named pipe injection.
author: Jay Kerai

title: List of MFA Methods Used with UPN Details
description: Enumerates all MFA methods in use across the tenant from SigninLogs, with user UPN details for each method.
author: Nathan Hutchinson
content:
// Title: List of MFA Methods Used with UPN Details
// Author: Nathan Hutchinson
// Source: https://www.kqlsearch.com
// KQL Library - https://github.com/sivolko/kql-hunter
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where ResultType == "0"
| where isnotempty(AuthenticationDetails)
| mv-expand AuthStep = parse_json(AuthenticationDetails)
| extend MFAMethod = tostring(AuthStep.authenticationMethod)
| extend MFAResult = tostring(AuthStep.succeeded)
| where MFAMethod != "Previously satisfied"
| where MFAResult == "true"
| summarize UserCount = dcount(UserPrincipalName), Users = make_set(UserPrincipalName, 10) by MFAMethod
| order by UserCount desc

title: MDXDR Critical Assets
description: Identifies critical assets in Microsoft Defender XDR using ExposureGraphNodes and IdentityInfo, highlighting high-value targets.
author: Alex Verboon
content:
// Title: MDXDR Critical Assets
// Author: Alex Verboon
// Source: https://www.kqlsearch.com
// KQL Library - https://github.com/sivolko/kql-hunter
ExposureGraphNodes
| where NodeLabel == "device"
| extend CriticalityLevel = tostring(NodeProperties.rawData.criticalityLevel)
| where CriticalityLevel in ("Critical", "High")
| extend AssetName = tostring(NodeProperties.rawData.displayName)
| extend OS = tostring(NodeProperties.rawData.operatingSystemPlatform)
| extend ExposureScore = todouble(NodeProperties.rawData.exposureScore)
| join kind=leftouter (IdentityInfo | summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated,*) by AccountObjectId | project AccountObjectId, AccountUpn, JobTitle, Department) on $left.NodeId == $right.AccountObjectId
| project AssetName, CriticalityLevel, OS, ExposureScore, AccountUpn, Department
| order by ExposureScore desc

title: Defender RedSun: TieringEngineService Created in AppData
description: Detects the RedSun TieringEngineService binary being created in AppData, a known indicator of the RedSun malware loader.
author: Jay Kerai

title: Suspicious Device Code Authentication
description: Detects suspicious device code authentication flows across SigninLogs, AADNonInteractiveUserSignInLogs, and ADFSSignInLogs that may indicate device code phishing.
author: Jose Sebastian Canos

title: Users Authenticating with the MFA Companion App
description: Identifies users signing in using the Microsoft Authenticator Companion app, useful for auditing non-standard MFA methods.
author: Nathan Hutchinson

title: Overview of All MFA Methods in Use
description: Provides a tenant-wide overview of all MFA authentication methods in use, with counts per method.
author: Nathan Hutchinson
content:
// Title: Overview of All MFA Methods in Use
// Author: Nathan Hutchinson
// Source: https://www.kqlsearch.com
// KQL Library - https://github.com/sivolko/kql-hunter
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where ResultType == "0"
| where isnotempty(AuthenticationDetails)
| mv-expand AuthStep = parse_json(AuthenticationDetails)
| extend MFAMethod = tostring(AuthStep.authenticationMethod)
| extend MFASucceeded = tostring(AuthStep.succeeded)
| where MFAMethod !in ("Previously satisfied", "Password")
| summarize
    UniqueUsers = dcount(UserPrincipalName),
    TotalSignIns = count(),
    SuccessRate = countif(MFASucceeded == "true") * 100 / count()
    by MFAMethod
| order by UniqueUsers desc

title: Suspicious 0-Day Adobe Reader Process Activity
description: Detects suspicious network connections and file writes by Adobe Reader processes, indicative of a potential zero-day exploitation attempt.
author: Benjamin Zulliger
content:
// Title: Suspicious 0-Day Adobe Reader Process Activity
// Author: Benjamin Zulliger
// Source: https://www.kqlsearch.com
// MITRE: T1203 - Exploitation for Client Execution
// KQL Library - https://github.com/sivolko/kql-hunter
let AdobeProcesses = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FileName in~ ("AcroRd32.exe", "Acrobat.exe", "AdobeARM.exe")
| project DeviceId, DeviceName, AdobePID = ProcessId, AdobeTime = Timestamp;
let SuspiciousNetwork = DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("AcroRd32.exe", "Acrobat.exe")
| where not(ipv4_is_private(RemoteIP))
| where RemotePort !in (80, 443)
| project DeviceId, NetTime = Timestamp, RemoteIP, RemotePort, RemoteUrl;
let SuspiciousFiles = DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("AcroRd32.exe", "Acrobat.exe")
| where FolderPath has_any ("Temp", "AppData", "Downloads")
| where FileName endswith_any (".exe", ".dll", ".ps1", ".bat")
| project DeviceId, FileTime = Timestamp, FileName, FolderPath, SHA256;
AdobeProcesses
| join kind=inner SuspiciousNetwork on DeviceId
| join kind=inner SuspiciousFiles on DeviceId
| where NetTime between (AdobeTime .. AdobeTime + 5m)
| where FileTime between (AdobeTime .. AdobeTime + 5m)
| project DeviceName, AdobeTime, RemoteIP, RemotePort, FileName, FolderPath, SHA256
| order by AdobeTime desc

title: Detect TLS Validation Bypass via PowerShell
description: Detects PowerShell commands that disable TLS certificate validation, a common technique used by malware to bypass SSL inspection.
author: Sergio Albea

title: Audit Claude AI Behavior on Endpoints
description: Detects process execution and file activity patterns associated with Claude AI (Anthropic) running on endpoints, for auditing AI tool usage.
author: Benjamin Zulliger

title: Git Force Push Detection
description: Detects git force push commands on endpoints, which can be used to rewrite repository history and cover tracks.
author: Ali Hussein

title: Git Commit --amend --no-verify Detection
description: Detects git commit amendment with the --no-verify flag, which bypasses pre-commit hooks and may be used to sneak in malicious code.
author: Ali Hussein

title: Batch Git Abuse Detection
description: Detects execution of multiple git abuse techniques in batch (cmd.exe or scripts) within a short time window on the same device.
author: Ali Hussein

title: Defense Evasion: Git Time Change
description: Detects system time changes occurring shortly before or after git commit activity, which may indicate an attempt to falsify commit timestamps.
author: Ali Hussein

title: Defense Evasion: Git Config Masquerade
description: Detects git config changes to user.name or user.email that may be used to masquerade as another developer when making commits.
author: Ali Hussein

title: Correlation: Git and VS Code Task Abuse
description: Correlates git abuse commands launched from VS Code tasks, which may indicate malicious use of IDE automation for supply chain tampering.
author: Ali Hussein

title: IFEO: Unauthorized Debugger Registration
description: Detects Image File Execution Options (IFEO) registry modifications that register unexpected debuggers, a common persistence and privilege escalation technique.
author: Benjamin Zulliger

title: macOS: Suspicious Shell or Process Execution from Browser
description: Detects shell or direct process spawning from browser processes on macOS, which may indicate exploitation of a browser vulnerability.
author: Benjamin Zulliger

title: Device TVM Secure Configuration Assessment Summary
description: Summarizes the Threat and Vulnerability Management (TVM) secure configuration assessment results per device, highlighting non-compliant configurations.
author: Benjamin Zulliger
content:
// Title: Device TVM Secure Configuration Assessment Summary
// Author: Benjamin Zulliger
// Source: https://www.kqlsearch.com
// KQL Library - https://github.com/sivolko/kql-hunter
DeviceTvmSecureConfigurationAssessment
| where Timestamp > ago(1d)
| where IsCompliant == false
| join kind=leftouter (DeviceTvmSecureConfigurationAssessmentKB | project ConfigurationId, ConfigurationName, ConfigurationDescription, RiskDescription, Tags)
    on ConfigurationId
| summarize
    FailedConfigs = dcount(ConfigurationId),
    FailedConfigNames = make_set(ConfigurationName, 10),
    HighRiskCount = countif(Tags has "high")
    by DeviceId, DeviceName
| order by FailedConfigs desc

title: Node C2 Polinder Detection
description: Detects suspicious outbound network connections from Node.js processes that may indicate C2 communication via a polinder-style payload.
author: Ali Hussein

title: Microsoft Defender Cloud App Discovery to Log Analytics
description: Exports Microsoft Defender Cloud App Discovery data to a custom Log Analytics table for extended retention and custom alerting.
author: Benjamin Zulliger
